title: JWT EXP Claim Explained
seoTitle: JWT exp Claim (Expiration Time) — Meaning & Validation
description: What is the JWT exp claim? Learn Expiration Time meaning, examples, and validation rules for exp in JSON Web Tokens.
keywords: jwt exp claim,exp jwt,expiration time jwt
content:
What Is the exp Claim?

The exp (Expiration Time) claim is a Unix timestamp indicating when the token must no longer be accepted.

Example

{ "exp": 1735689600 }

How to Inspect

Paste any JWT into JWT Decoder to see the exp claim in the decoded payload. Use JWT Debugger for validation warnings related to this claim.

Validation Rules

Reject tokens where exp is in the past. Allow small clock skew (30s) server-side.

faq.a: See this page for a full explanation with examples and validation rules.

faq.a: Paste your JWT into our free JWT Decoder to see all claims in the payload.

title: JWT SUB Claim Explained
description: What is the JWT sub claim? Learn Subject meaning, examples, and validation rules for sub in JSON Web Tokens.
content:
What Is the sub Claim?

The sub (Subject) claim identifies the principal (user or entity) that is the subject of the token.

Example

{ "sub": "user-123" }

How to Inspect

Paste any JWT into JWT Decoder to see the sub claim in the decoded payload. Use JWT Debugger for validation warnings related to this claim.

Validation Rules

Must match the expected user ID. This is the primary identity claim.

title: JWT ISS Claim Explained
description: What is the JWT iss claim? Learn Issuer meaning, examples, and validation rules for iss in JSON Web Tokens.
content:
What Is the iss Claim?

The iss (Issuer) claim identifies the principal that issued the JWT, typically your auth server URL.

Example

{ "iss": "https://auth.example.com" }

How to Inspect

Paste any JWT into JWT Decoder to see the iss claim in the decoded payload. Use JWT Debugger for validation warnings related to this claim.

Validation Rules

Must match your configured issuer URL exactly.

title: JWT AUD Claim Explained
description: What is the JWT aud claim? Learn Audience meaning, examples, and validation rules for aud in JSON Web Tokens.
content:
What Is the aud Claim?

The aud (Audience) claim identifies the recipients that the JWT is intended for — your API or client ID.

Example

{ "aud": "api.example.com" }

How to Inspect

Paste any JWT into JWT Decoder to see the aud claim in the decoded payload. Use JWT Debugger for validation warnings related to this claim.

Validation Rules

Must include your API identifier or client ID.

title: JWT IAT Claim Explained
seoTitle: JWT iat Claim (Issued At) — Meaning & Validation
description: What is the JWT iat claim? Learn Issued At meaning, examples, and validation rules for iat in JSON Web Tokens.
keywords: jwt iat claim,iat jwt,issued at jwt
content:
What Is the iat Claim?

The iat (Issued At) claim is a Unix timestamp of when the token was created.

Example

{ "iat": 1700000000 }

How to Inspect

Paste any JWT into JWT Decoder to see the iat claim in the decoded payload. Use JWT Debugger for validation warnings related to this claim.

Validation Rules

Reject tokens issued too far in the future (clock skew).

title: JWT NBF Claim Explained
seoTitle: JWT nbf Claim (Not Before) — Meaning & Validation
description: What is the JWT nbf claim? Learn Not Before meaning, examples, and validation rules for nbf in JSON Web Tokens.
keywords: jwt nbf claim,nbf jwt,not before jwt
content:
What Is the nbf Claim?

The nbf (Not Before) claim is a Unix timestamp before which the token must not be accepted.

Example

{ "nbf": 1700000000 }

How to Inspect

Paste any JWT into JWT Decoder to see the nbf claim in the decoded payload. Use JWT Debugger for validation warnings related to this claim.

Validation Rules

Reject tokens where current time is before nbf.

title: JWT JTI Claim Explained
seoTitle: JWT jti Claim (JWT ID) — Meaning & Validation
description: What is the JWT jti claim? Learn JWT ID meaning, examples, and validation rules for jti in JSON Web Tokens.
keywords: jwt jti claim,jti jwt,jwt id jwt
content:
What Is the jti Claim?

The jti (JWT ID) claim provides a unique identifier for the token, used for revocation and replay prevention.

Example

{ "jti": "unique-token-id" }

How to Inspect

Paste any JWT into JWT Decoder to see the jti claim in the decoded payload. Use JWT Debugger for validation warnings related to this claim.

Validation Rules

Use with a revocation list to prevent token replay.
